# fuzzymatch — github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch — Full API Reference

> This document mirrors `llms.txt` with full Go signatures, per-symbol
> godoc summaries, per-AlgoID primary-source citations, per-error full
> message text, and per-options-struct field tables. Verified in sync
> with the root package via `ai_friendly_test.go`, which parses every
> exported root-package symbol via `go/ast` and asserts each appears in
> `llms.txt`. The full document below is editorial detail layered on
> top of the concise index.

## Overview

A pure-Go library for fuzzy string matching:

- 23 string-similarity algorithms (Phase 2+).
- A weighted composite `Scorer` (Phase 8).
- A collection-scan sub-package at `github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch/scan`
  (Phase 9).
- A one-to-many `Extract` search API (Phase 10).

Constraints (locked):

- Go 1.26.3 minimum.
- Apache-2.0 throughout.
- No cgo anywhere.
- Zero non-stdlib runtime deps in the root `go.mod` except
  `golang.org/x/text` (Unicode normalisation only). The allowlist is
  enforced by `make verify-deps-allowlist` in CI.
- No goroutines / channels / mutexes in library code.
- No init()-time table builds.
- No map iteration on output paths.
- No transcendental float operations on output paths (DET-06).
- Cross-platform byte-identical output (linux amd64+arm64, darwin
  amd64+arm64, windows amd64) verified by golden-file tests.

## Public API (root package: fuzzymatch)

The four primitive surfaces below land in Phase 1. The 23 algorithm
score functions (e.g. `LevenshteinScore`), the `Scorer`, the `scan`
sub-package, and the `Extract` API land in subsequent phases.

### AlgoID dispatch enum (algoid.go)

```go
// AlgoID is the typed enum that identifies each of the 23 algorithms in
// the fuzzymatch catalogue. It is a plain int — not int32, not int64,
// not a struct — so that the package-internal dispatch table can index
// into it directly with zero allocation overhead.
type AlgoID int
```

```go
// String returns the canonical CamelCase spelling of the algorithm.
// Stable across patch releases.
func (id AlgoID) String() string
```

```go
// AlgoIDs returns the full set of 23 algorithm identifiers in their
// declared order (the v1.x stable catalogue order from
// docs/requirements.md §7).
func AlgoIDs() []AlgoID
```

### AlgoID constants and primary sources

| Constant | String() | Primary source |
|----------|----------|----------------|
| `AlgoLevenshtein` | "Levenshtein" | Levenshtein, V. I. (1965). "Binary codes capable of correcting deletions, insertions, and reversals." *Soviet Physics Doklady*, 10(8):707–710. |
| `AlgoDamerauLevenshteinOSA` | "DamerauLevenshteinOSA" | Boytsov, L. (2011). "Indexing methods for approximate dictionary searching: comparative analysis." *ACM JEA*, 16, Art. 1; Damerau, F. J. (1964). *CACM*, 7(3):171–176. |
| `AlgoDamerauLevenshteinFull` | "DamerauLevenshteinFull" | Lowrance, R., Wagner, R. A. (1975). "An extension of the string-to-string correction problem." *JACM*, 22(2):177–183. |
| `AlgoHamming` | "Hamming" | Hamming, R. W. (1950). "Error detecting and error correcting codes." *Bell System Tech. J.*, 29(2):147–160. |
| `AlgoJaro` | "Jaro" | Jaro, M. A. (1989). *JASA*, 84(406):414–420. |
| `AlgoJaroWinkler` | "JaroWinkler" | Winkler, W. E. (1990). *Proc. Section on Survey Research Methods*, ASA: 354–359. |
| `AlgoStrcmp95` | "Strcmp95" | Winkler, W. E. (1994). *Proc. Section on Survey Research Methods*, ASA: 467–472. U.S. Census Bureau (1995) `strcmp95.c` reference. |
| `AlgoSmithWatermanGotoh` | "SmithWatermanGotoh" | Smith, T. F., Waterman, M. S. (1981). *J. Mol. Biol.*, 147(1):195–197; Gotoh, O. (1982). *J. Mol. Biol.*, 162(3):705–708. |
| `AlgoLCSStr` | "LCSStr" | Wagner, R. A., Fischer, M. J. (1974). *JACM*, 21(1):168–173. |
| `AlgoQGramJaccard` | "QGramJaccard" | Ukkonen, E. (1992). *TCS*, 92(1):191–211; Jaccard, P. (1912). *New Phytologist*, 11(2):37–50. |
| `AlgoSorensenDice` | "SorensenDice" | Dice, L. R. (1945). *Ecology*, 26(3):297–302; Sørensen, T. (1948). *KDVS*, 5(4):1–34. |
| `AlgoCosine` | "Cosine" | Salton, G., McGill, M. J. (1983). *Introduction to Modern Information Retrieval*. McGraw-Hill. |
| `AlgoTversky` | "Tversky" | Tversky, A. (1977). "Features of similarity." *Psychological Review*, 84(4):327–352. |
| `AlgoMongeElkan` | "MongeElkan" | Monge, A. E., Elkan, C. P. (1996). *KDD-96*: 267–270. |
| `AlgoTokenSortRatio` | "TokenSortRatio" | SeatGeek (2014) *fuzzywuzzy*; modern reference RapidFuzz (Bachmann, M., 2020–). |
| `AlgoTokenSetRatio` | "TokenSetRatio" | As Token Sort Ratio. |
| `AlgoPartialRatio` | "PartialRatio" | As Token Sort Ratio. |
| `AlgoTokenJaccard` | "TokenJaccard" | Jaccard, P. (1912) (as Q-Gram Jaccard, applied to word tokens). |
| `AlgoSoundex` | "Soundex" | Russell, R. C., Odell, M. K. (1918, 1922). U.S. Patents 1261167 and 1435663. Algorithm description: Knuth (1973) *TAOCP* Vol. 3 §6.4. |
| `AlgoDoubleMetaphone` | "DoubleMetaphone" | Philips, L. (2000). *C/C++ Users Journal*, 18(6):38–43. |
| `AlgoNYSIIS` | "NYSIIS" | Taft, R. L. (1970). *Name search techniques*. NY State Special Report No. 1, Albany. |
| `AlgoMRA` | "MRA" | Moore et al. (1977). *Accessing individual records from personal data files…*. NBS Tech. Note 943. |
| `AlgoRatcliffObershelp` | "RatcliffObershelp" | Ratcliff, J. W., Metzener, D. E. (1988). *Dr. Dobb's Journal*, 13(7):46–51. |

Metaphone 3 (Philips 2009, U.S. Patent 7,440,941) is **explicitly EXCLUDED**. See `docs/faq.md` for the rationale.

### Character-tier foundation surface (Levenshtein, Damerau-Levenshtein OSA / Full, Hamming, Jaro, Jaro-Winkler)

The six foundation character-tier algorithms each ship a byte path (`*Score`, `*Distance`) plus a rune-aware variant (`*ScoreRunes`, `*DistanceRunes`). Bytes are the ASCII fast path; runes are the Unicode-correct path. Every signature is symmetric across argument order (true metrics) and returns deterministic byte-identical output across the five-platform CI matrix.

```go
// LevenshteinDistance returns the Levenshtein edit distance between a
// and b in bytes (insertions, deletions, substitutions all at unit
// cost). True metric; symmetric across argument order. Wagner-Fischer
// 1974 two-row DP; ASCII fast path for inputs ≤ 64 bytes.
func LevenshteinDistance(a, b string) int

// LevenshteinDistanceRunes returns the Levenshtein edit distance under
// rune semantics — required for correct Unicode results on multi-byte
// codepoints.
func LevenshteinDistanceRunes(a, b string) int

// LevenshteinScore returns 1 - LevenshteinDistance(a, b) / max(len(a),
// len(b)) in [0.0, 1.0]; both-empty → 1.0 (identity short-circuit).
func LevenshteinScore(a, b string) float64

// LevenshteinScoreRunes returns the rune-aware Levenshtein similarity.
func LevenshteinScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// DamerauLevenshteinOSADistance returns the Optimal String Alignment
// Damerau-Levenshtein distance: Levenshtein + adjacent-transposition
// at unit cost, with the OSA restriction that each substring may not
// be modified more than once. NOT a true metric (triangle inequality
// can fail). Boytsov 2011 + Damerau 1964.
func DamerauLevenshteinOSADistance(a, b string) int
func DamerauLevenshteinOSADistanceRunes(a, b string) int
func DamerauLevenshteinOSAScore(a, b string) float64
func DamerauLevenshteinOSAScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// DamerauLevenshteinFullDistance returns the unrestricted Damerau-
// Levenshtein distance (Lowrance & Wagner 1975). TRUE METRIC: triangle
// inequality holds; symmetric; identity property satisfied. More
// expensive than OSA — uses a 2D DP with a "last seen" auxiliary
// array; allocations are O(m+n) on the heap. Use for academic
// correctness; use OSA for general-purpose typo correction.
func DamerauLevenshteinFullDistance(a, b string) int
func DamerauLevenshteinFullDistanceRunes(a, b string) int
func DamerauLevenshteinFullScore(a, b string) float64
func DamerauLevenshteinFullScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// HammingDistance returns the Hamming distance between a and b
// (Hamming 1950) — the number of positions at which the two byte
// sequences differ.
//
// SILENT-MAX POLICY (Phase 8.5 Q1 LOCKED): on unequal-length input,
// HammingDistance returns max(len(a), len(b)) WITHOUT error or panic.
// The earlier draft spec proposed an ErrUnequalLength error sentinel;
// Phase 8.5 Q1 catches the spec up to the shipped code — the data-vs-
// parameter framework (docs/requirements.md §6.A) classifies the two
// input strings as comparison data, which is handled leniently.
// Consumers wanting an explicit "unequal-length" signal call
// fuzzymatch.Validate(a, b) and check for WarnUnequalLength scoped to
// AlgoHamming.
func HammingDistance(a, b string) int

// HammingDistanceRunes returns the rune-aware Hamming distance under
// the same silent-max policy.
func HammingDistanceRunes(a, b string) int

// HammingScore returns 1 - HammingDistance(a, b) / max(len(a),
// len(b)) in [0.0, 1.0]. On unequal-length input the score is 0.0
// (silent-max policy). Both-empty → 1.0 (identity short-circuit).
func HammingScore(a, b string) float64

// HammingScoreRunes returns the rune-aware Hamming similarity under
// the same silent-max policy.
func HammingScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// JaroScore returns the Jaro similarity (Jaro 1989) in [0.0, 1.0].
// Symmetric across argument order. Both-empty → 1.0; one-empty → 0.0
// (the catalogue's vacuous-identity convention; differs from
// jellyfish 1.2.1 which returns 0.0 for jaro_similarity("", "") —
// variant_divergence documented in
// testdata/cross-validation/character/vectors.json).
func JaroScore(a, b string) float64
func JaroScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// JaroWinklerScore returns Jaro-Winkler (Winkler 1990) — Jaro plus a
// prefix bonus capped at the first 4 characters. Heavily used as a
// signal for left-anchored identifier matching; included in
// DefaultScorer.
func JaroWinklerScore(a, b string) float64
func JaroWinklerScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 4 algorithm surface (character + gestalt)

The Phase 4 plans land seven new exported functions across three
algorithms (Strcmp95 — Winkler 1994; LCSStr — Wagner-Fischer 1974;
Ratcliff-Obershelp — Dr. Dobb's 1988). One-line rationales:

```go
// Strcmp95Score returns Winkler 1994's enhanced similarity in [0, 1].
// Layers similar-character credit + prefix boost + long-string
// adjustment atop Jaro-Winkler. ASCII-only — no *Runes variant per the
// Winkler 1994 paper's letter-pair table. Strcmp95Score(a, b) >=
// JaroWinklerScore(a, b) holds on every input (hierarchy invariant).
func Strcmp95Score(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// LongestCommonSubstring returns the longest contiguous common substring
// of a and b (byte path; leftmost-in-a on ties). Empty string on no
// overlap or one-empty input.
func LongestCommonSubstring(a, b string) string
```

```go
// LongestCommonSubstringRunes returns the longest contiguous common
// substring of a and b under rune semantics (multi-byte UTF-8 safe).
// Leftmost-in-a on ties.
func LongestCommonSubstringRunes(a, b string) string
```

```go
// LCSStrScore returns the Sørensen-Dice-normalised longest-common-
// substring similarity in [0, 1]: 2·len(lcs) / (len(a) + len(b)). Byte
// path; ASCII fast-path zero-alloc on inputs <= 64 bytes.
func LCSStrScore(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// LCSStrScoreRunes returns the LCSStr score under rune semantics. Use
// for multi-byte UTF-8 input where codepoint-level matching is required.
func LCSStrScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// RatcliffObershelpScore returns the Ratcliff-Obershelp gestalt-
// pattern-matching similarity in [0, 1], byte-for-byte equivalent to
// Python's difflib.SequenceMatcher(autojunk=False).ratio() within 1e-9.
// INTENTIONALLY asymmetric in argument order — mirrors difflib's
// noncommutative ratio() per CPython bpo-37004 (OQ-1 resolution locked
// 2026-05-14): RatcliffObershelpScore(a, b) may differ from
// RatcliffObershelpScore(b, a).
func RatcliffObershelpScore(a, b string) float64
```

```go
// RatcliffObershelpScoreRunes returns the Ratcliff-Obershelp score
// under rune semantics. Identity short-circuit fires BEFORE []rune
// allocation; zero-alloc on the identity path.
func RatcliffObershelpScoreRunes(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 5 algorithm surface (q-gram tier — foundation + Jaccard)

The Phase 5 plan 05-01 lands the shared q-gram extraction infrastructure
(unexported `extractQGrams` / `extractQGramsRunes` per CONTEXT.md §2 LOCKED
— internal only, no public q-gram helper in v1) and the first algorithmic
consumer: Q-Gram Jaccard (Ukkonen 1992 §3 / Jaccard 1912). Two new
exported functions plus two new error sentinels (the latter consumed by
the Phase 8 Scorer options across all four q-gram algorithms).

```go
// QGramJaccardScore returns the multiset Jaccard similarity of the
// q-gram bags of a and b: J(A, B) = |QA ∩ QB| / |QA ∪ QB|, in [0, 1].
// Operates on bytes; UTF-8 inputs split q-grams at byte boundaries.
// Identity short-circuit (a == b) covers both-empty. Panics with
// "fuzzymatch: invalid q-gram size" when n < 1 — the Phase 8 Scorer
// option WithQGramJaccardAlgorithm returns ErrInvalidQGramSize instead.
// Reference: QGramJaccardScore("AGCT", "AGCTAGCT", 2) = 3/7 ≈ 0.4286.
func QGramJaccardScore(a, b string, n int) float64
```

```go
// QGramJaccardScoreRunes returns the multiset Jaccard similarity under
// rune semantics. Multi-byte UTF-8 windows are compared atomically;
// "café" decomposes to four runes and produces three rune-bigrams at
// n=2. Allocates two []rune slices on the heap; for ASCII inputs prefer
// QGramJaccardScore. Same panic-on-n<1 contract.
func QGramJaccardScoreRunes(a, b string, n int) float64
```

### Phase 5 algorithm surface (q-gram tier — Sørensen-Dice)

Plan 05-02 lands Sørensen-Dice (Dice 1945 §3 / Sørensen 1948 §3 —
independent rediscoveries) as the second q-gram-tier algorithm. Builds
on the shared `extractQGrams` / `extractQGramsRunes` helpers from plan
05-01. Two new exported functions plus a third dispatch slot
(AlgoSorensenDice) registered with the default-n=3 trigram wrapper per
CONTEXT.md Deferred §4. Sørensen-Dice weights the intersection more
heavily than Jaccard (DSC = 2·|∩|/(|QA|+|QB|) vs J = |∩|/|∪|), which
is the right default for many fuzzy name-matching workloads.

```go
// SorensenDiceScore returns the Sørensen-Dice coefficient of the
// q-gram multisets of a and b: DSC(A, B) = 2·|QA ∩ QB| / (|QA| + |QB|),
// in [0, 1]. Operates on bytes; UTF-8 inputs split q-grams at byte
// boundaries. Identity short-circuit (a == b) covers both-empty.
// Panics with "fuzzymatch: invalid q-gram size" when n < 1 — the
// Phase 8 Scorer option WithSorensenDiceAlgorithm returns
// ErrInvalidQGramSize instead.
// Reference: SorensenDiceScore("night", "nacht", 2) = 0.25.
func SorensenDiceScore(a, b string, n int) float64
```

```go
// SorensenDiceScoreRunes returns the Sørensen-Dice coefficient under
// rune semantics. Multi-byte UTF-8 windows are compared atomically;
// "café" decomposes to four runes and produces three rune-bigrams at
// n=2. Allocates two []rune slices on the heap; for ASCII inputs
// prefer SorensenDiceScore. Same panic-on-n<1 contract.
// Reference: SorensenDiceScoreRunes("café", "cafe", 2) = 4/6 ≈ 0.6667.
func SorensenDiceScoreRunes(a, b string, n int) float64
```

### Phase 5 algorithm surface (q-gram tier — Cosine)

Plan 05-03 lands Cosine n-gram similarity (Salton & McGill 1983 §4.1
eq. 4.4 p.121) as the third q-gram-tier algorithm. Builds on the
shared `extractQGrams` / `extractQGramsRunes` helpers from plan 05-01.
Two new exported functions plus a fourth dispatch slot (AlgoCosine)
registered with the default-n=3 trigram wrapper per CONTEXT.md
Deferred §4. Cosine is the LOAD-BEARING cross-platform float-determinism
algorithm for Phase 5: the algorithms.json golden file Cosine entries
(merged into algorithms.json by plan 05-05) are the gate that detects
any drift in float reduction order across the four CI platforms
(linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/arm64, windows/amd64).

The implementation uses sort.Strings on the intersection key slice
(CONTEXT.md §3 LOCKED) before the dot-product reduction loop;
math.Sqrt on integer-derived sums-of-squares for the norms (IEEE-754
correctly rounded on all four CI platforms per RESEARCH.md §3.5);
explicit `(x*y) + z` parenthesisation per DET-06; and `dot / (normA *
normB)` factorised cosine form (Salton & McGill 1983 §4.1) for the
final division. NO math.Pow / math.Log / math.Exp / math.FMA — only
math.Sqrt.

```go
// CosineScore returns the Cosine similarity of the q-gram frequency
// vectors of a and b: cos(A, B) = (A · B) / (‖A‖ × ‖B‖), in [0, 1]
// (Salton & McGill 1983 §4.1 eq. 4.4 p.121). Operates on bytes; UTF-8
// inputs split q-grams at byte boundaries. Identity short-circuit
// (a == b) covers both-empty. Iteration order over intersection keys
// is sorted (sort.Strings) per CONTEXT.md §3 LOCKED. Panics with
// "fuzzymatch: invalid q-gram size" when n < 1 — the Phase 8 Scorer
// option WithCosineAlgorithm returns ErrInvalidQGramSize instead.
// Reference: CosineScore("abc", "abcd", 2) = 2/sqrt(6) ≈ 0.81649658
// (the IEEE-754 actual from the factorised form is 1 ULP from this
// rational limit; see cosine_test.go RV-C1 for the precision
// discussion).
func CosineScore(a, b string, n int) float64
```

```go
// CosineScoreRunes returns the Cosine similarity of the q-gram
// frequency vectors of a and b under rune semantics. Multi-byte UTF-8
// windows are compared atomically; "café" decomposes to four runes
// and produces three rune-bigrams at n=2. Allocates two []rune slices
// on the heap plus the two multiset maps and the intersection key
// slice. For ASCII inputs prefer CosineScore. Same panic-on-n<1
// contract.
// Reference: CosineScoreRunes("café", "cafe", 2) = 2/3 ≈ 0.66666667
// (1 ULP from the rational limit per the factorised form).
func CosineScoreRunes(a, b string, n int) float64
```

### Phase 5 algorithm surface (q-gram tier — Tversky)

Plan 05-04 lands Tversky asymmetric similarity (Tversky 1977 §2 eq. (1)
p. 332) as the fourth and final q-gram-tier algorithm. Builds on the
shared `extractQGrams` / `extractQGramsRunes` helpers from plan 05-01.
Two new exported functions plus a fifth dispatch slot (AlgoTversky)
registered with a default-n=3, α=β=1.0 (Jaccard-equivalent) wrapper per
CONTEXT.md "Claude's Discretion" — the dispatch signature has no place
for n, α, or β, so the real direction-sensitive use case lands in
Phase 8 via WithTverskyAlgorithm(weight, alpha, beta).

Tversky is the parameterised q-gram metric:
T(A, B, α, β) = |A∩B| / (|A∩B| + α·|A−B| + β·|B−A|). With α ≠ β the
function is direction-sensitive — swapping (a, b) with fixed (α, β)
generally yields a DIFFERENT score (the load-bearing RV-T1 / RV-T2 pair
asserts this: 0.8823 vs 0.6521 for inputs swapped). With α = β = 1 the
function reduces to Q-Gram Jaccard (bit-for-bit equality on the same
multisets); with α = β = 0.5 it reduces to Sørensen-Dice (also bit-for-
bit). The asymmetry is exercised at THREE layers: unit test
(TestTversky_AsymmetryDirectionSensitive in tversky_test.go), property
test (TestProp_TverskyScore_AsymmetricWhenAlphaNeqBeta + the
ParameterSwapSymmetry algebraic identity), AND the Gherkin asymmetry
direction-sensitivity scenario in tests/bdd/features/tversky.feature.

```go
// TverskyScore returns the Tversky asymmetric similarity of the q-gram
// multisets of a and b: T(A, B, α, β) = |QA ∩ QB| / (|QA ∩ QB| +
// α·|QA − QB| + β·|QB − QA|), in [0, 1] (Tversky 1977 §2 eq. (1)
// p. 332). Operates on bytes; UTF-8 inputs split q-grams at byte
// boundaries. Identity short-circuit (a == b) covers both-empty.
// Symmetric when α = β; direction-sensitive when α ≠ β. Special cases:
// α=β=1.0 reduces to Q-Gram Jaccard (bit-for-bit); α=β=0.5 reduces to
// Sørensen-Dice (bit-for-bit). Panics with "fuzzymatch: invalid q-gram
// size" when n < 1; panics with "fuzzymatch: invalid tversky parameter"
// when α < 0, β < 0, or α + β == 0 — the Phase 8 Scorer option
// WithTverskyAlgorithm returns ErrInvalidQGramSize / ErrInvalidTverskyParam
// instead. The α/β surface is the asymmetric-similarity feature; the
// dispatch table wraps with α=β=1.0 (Jaccard-equivalent) as a
// deliberate compromise — see dispatch_tversky.go.
// Reference (load-bearing asymmetry pair):
//   TverskyScore("abcd",   "abcdef", 2, 0.8, 0.2) = 0.8823529411764706
//   TverskyScore("abcdef", "abcd",   2, 0.8, 0.2) = 0.6521739130434783
func TverskyScore(a, b string, n int, alpha, beta float64) float64
```

```go
// TverskyScoreRunes returns the Tversky asymmetric similarity of the
// q-gram multisets of a and b under rune semantics. Multi-byte UTF-8
// windows are compared atomically; "café" decomposes to four runes
// and produces three rune-bigrams at n=2. Allocates two []rune slices
// on the heap plus the two multiset maps. For ASCII inputs prefer
// TverskyScore. Same panic contracts (n<1; α<0/β<0/α+β==0). Same
// asymmetry surface — the rune path is the rune-aware twin, NOT a
// symmetric variant.
// Reference: TverskyScoreRunes("café", "cafe", 2, 0.5, 0.5) = 2/3 ≈ 0.66667.
func TverskyScoreRunes(a, b string, n int, alpha, beta float64) float64
```

### Phase 6 algorithm surface (token tier — TokenSortRatio)

Plan 06-01 lands the shared `token_indel.go` foundation kernel
(unexported `lcsLen` / `indelRatio` / `lcsLenRunes` / `indelRatioRunes`
helpers per 06-CONTEXT.md §2 LOCKED — internal only, no public
LCS-subsequence or Indel-ratio helper in v1) and the first token-tier
algorithm: TokenSortRatio. The kernel is the LCS-subsequence
Wagner-Fischer 1974 DP wrapped in RapidFuzz's canonical "Indel"
normalisation 2·LCS / (|a|+|b|), shared by TokenSortRatio (this plan),
TokenSetRatio (plan 06-02), and PartialRatio (plan 06-03).

TokenSortRatio composes against the kernel via the simplest possible
pipeline: tokenise both sides using `DefaultTokeniseOptions()`
(Lowercase: true, SplitCamelCase: true, SplitConsecutiveUpper: true,
SeparatorChars: `"_-.:/ \t\n\r"`), sort each token slice byte-lex
ascending via `sort.Strings`, join with a single space, then apply
`indelRatio` over the byte slices of the two joined strings.
Per-plan llms-sync discipline (per 06-CONTEXT.md §6 reinforcement)
lands the symbol and full godoc-style description in the same
plan that introduces TokenSortRatioScore.

Tokeniser-divergence note (OQ-1 RESOLUTION LOCKED in plan 06-01):
RapidFuzz tokenises via Python `str.split()` (whitespace-only,
case-preserving); fuzzymatch's `Tokenise` is identifier-aware
(camelCase / snake_case / kebab-case / dot-case + lowercasing). For
whitespace-only lowercase ASCII inputs the two agree and the
cross-validation corpus at
`testdata/cross-validation/token-ratios/vectors.json` asserts
byte-stable agreement against RapidFuzz 3.14.5 within `epsilon = 1e-9`.
For inputs with mixed identifier styles the project tokenisation
produces semantically richer splits.

```go
// TokenSortRatioScore returns the Token Sort Ratio similarity between
// a and b in [0.0, 1.0]: tokenise both sides using
// DefaultTokeniseOptions(), sort each token slice byte-lex ascending,
// join with a single space, then apply the Indel formula
// 2·LCS / (|joinedA|+|joinedB|) (Wagner & Fischer 1974
// LCS-subsequence DP over bytes; RapidFuzz-canonical normalisation).
// Conventions: both-empty / identity → 1.0 (a == b short-circuit
// covers both); one-empty → 0.0; token-reorder → 1.0 (sorted-joined
// strings identical). Symmetric across argument order — bit-for-bit
// equality, no float tolerance needed.
//
// Reference vector:
//   TokenSortRatioScore("fuzzy wuzzy was a bear", "wuzzy fuzzy was a bear") = 1.0
func TokenSortRatioScore(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 6 algorithm surface (token tier — TokenSetRatio)

Plan 06-02 lands the second Indel-formula consumer of the shared
`token_indel.go` kernel: TokenSetRatio. The construction is more
complex than TokenSortRatio's tokenise-sort-join pipeline:
TokenSetRatio dedupes each token list to a set, computes the sorted
intersection / diff_a_to_b / diff_b_to_a slices, subset-short-circuits
when one set is a subset of the other, and otherwise takes the max
of three Indel ratios over the constructed-string forms (intersection
vs intersection+diff_ab, intersection vs intersection+diff_ba,
intersection+diff_ab vs intersection+diff_ba).

DEVIATION from the catalogue both-empty → 1.0 convention (LOCKED in
plan 06-02): TokenSetRatioScore returns 0.0 (NOT 1.0) when either
input is empty OR when either tokenised input is empty — bug-for-bug
compatibility with RapidFuzz issue #110 / fuzzywuzzy. The empty-input
gate fires BEFORE the identity short-circuit so
TokenSetRatioScore("", "") returns 0.0 (matching RapidFuzz), NOT 1.0.
The identity short-circuit (`if a == b { return 1.0 }`) only fires
for NON-EMPTY identical strings. The post-Tokenise empty-set gate
catches the remaining edge: raw strings non-empty but both tokenise
empty (e.g. `(" ", "  ")` → 0.0). Other tokenised algorithms in the
catalogue (TokenJaccard, MongeElkan) follow the standard convention;
TokenSetRatio is the documented exception, recorded in the
algorithm godoc, BDD scenarios, the staging-golden test case, the
cross-validation corpus, and `docs/cross-validation.md`.

DoS notice (per 06-CONTEXT.md §5 LOCKED): the third (combined-vs-
combined) branch is the worst-case cost driver — O((|combined1to2| +
|combined2to1|)^2) for the LCS DP. On asymmetric-cardinality inputs
(5-token vs 100-token, 2-token shared core), the DP performs ~10^4
cell updates. See `BenchmarkTokenSetRatio_Pathological_AsymmetricSetCardinalities`
for measured timings on representative pathological shapes.

```go
// TokenSetRatioScore returns the Token Set Ratio similarity between
// a and b in [0.0, 1.0]: tokenise both sides using
// DefaultTokeniseOptions(), compute the sorted intersection +
// diff_ab + diff_ba token slices, subset-short-circuit when one set
// is a subset of the other (AND the intersection is non-empty), or
// otherwise take the max of three Indel ratios over the
// constructed-string forms (intersection vs intersection+diff_ab,
// intersection vs intersection+diff_ba, intersection+diff_ab vs
// intersection+diff_ba).
//
// DEVIATION (LOCKED — RapidFuzz issue #110 bug-for-bug): when
// either input is empty OR either Tokenise output is empty, the
// function returns 0.0 (NOT 1.0). The empty-input gate fires
// BEFORE the identity short-circuit so ("", "") → 0.0 (matching
// RapidFuzz). Identity short-circuit fires only for non-empty
// identical strings.
//
// Reference vector:
//   TokenSetRatioScore("hello world", "world peace") = 7/11 ≈ 0.6364
//   (three-way max where the combined-vs-combined branch wins).
func TokenSetRatioScore(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 6 algorithm surface (token tier — PartialRatio)

Plan 06-03 lands the third Indel-formula consumer of the shared
`token_indel.go` kernel: PartialRatio. PartialRatio is the only Phase 6
algorithm with BOTH byte and rune surfaces per spec lines 609-610 and
the only Phase 6 algorithm that does NOT tokenise its inputs — it
operates at the character level, sliding the shorter input across all
alignments of the longer input.

The algorithm uses the **three-region iteration** pattern transcribed
structurally from RapidFuzz's MIT-licensed `fuzz_py.py::_partial_ratio_impl`
(06-RESEARCH.md Pattern 6 + Example 4):

- **Region 1 (left tail):** `for i := 1; i < m; i++` — substrings
  `longer[:i]` of length 1..m-1. The left edge of the shorter input
  "hangs off" the start of the longer input.
- **Region 2 (middle):** `for i := 0; i <= n-m; i++` — full m-length
  windows `longer[i:i+m]`. The standard sliding-window case.
- **Region 3 (right tail):** `for i := n-m; i < n; i++` (guarded by
  `n > m`) — substrings `longer[i:]` of length n-i down to 1. The
  right edge "hangs off" the end of the longer input.

Plus the **`s1_char_set` early-skip optimisation**: a `[256]bool` (byte
path) or `map[rune]struct{}` (rune path) of bytes/runes appearing in
the shorter input. Alignments whose last (Region 1/2) or first
(Region 3) byte/rune is not in the set are pruned — load-bearing for
the pathological budget per 06-RESEARCH.md Pitfall 3.

PITFALL 3 (06-RESEARCH.md): a naive single-loop implementation
`for i := 0; i <= n-m; i++` covers only Region 2. The keystone
fixtures `PartialRatioScore("abc", "ab") = 1.0` (Region 1 left tail)
and `PartialRatioScore("abc", "bc") = 1.0` (Region 3 right tail) are
pinned in unit tests, BDD scenarios, AND the cross-validation corpus
to prevent regression to the naive implementation.

Surface: PartialRatio ships ONLY `PartialRatioScore(a, b string) float64`
(byte path; registered in `dispatch[AlgoPartialRatio]`). The rune
variant `PartialRatioScoreRunes` was REMOVED in Phase 8.5 Q5 — token-
tier algorithms operate on the output of `Tokenise`, which is itself
rune-aware (each emitted token is a complete code-point sequence), so
the byte-level Indel kernel produces correct results on Unicode
inputs without a separate rune surface.

DOES NOT inherit TokenSetRatio's RapidFuzz issue #110 deviation —
PartialRatio follows the catalogue's standard both-empty → 1.0
convention because the algorithm has a meaningful interpretation at
the character level even when both inputs are empty.

DoS notice (per 06-CONTEXT.md §5 LOCKED): worst-case complexity is
O(|s|·|l|·max(|s|,|l|)). On long-vs-short mismatches (~10 chars vs
~10,000 chars) the middle region performs ~10,000 indelRatio calls,
each ~100 LCS DP cell updates — the char-set early-skip prunes ~95%
of these calls when the shared alphabet is small. See
`BenchmarkPartialRatio_Pathological_LongShortMismatch_Bytes` for
measured timings.

Future optimisation (spec-deferred to v1.x): spec line 612 explicitly
defers the O(|s|·|l|) sliding-window DP variant to v1.x. Phase 6 ships
the straightforward loop-over-alignments + indelRatio approach with
the s1_char_set early-skip. A `TODO(#TBD)` in `partial_ratio.go`
tracks the future GitHub issue for the sliding-window DP
implementation.

```go
// PartialRatioScore returns the Partial Ratio similarity between a
// and b in [0.0, 1.0]: the maximum Indel-formula similarity ratio
// over alignments of the shorter input against substrings of the
// longer input, computed via the three-region iteration (Region 1
// left tail / Region 2 middle / Region 3 right tail) with the
// `s1_char_set` early-skip. RapidFuzz-canonical normalisation
// 2·LCS / (|substr_a|+|substr_b|).
// Conventions: both-empty / identity → 1.0; one-empty → 0.0;
// Region-1 / Region-2 / Region-3 alignment that produces a perfect
// substring match → 1.0. Symmetric across argument order.
//
// Reference vectors (cross-validated against RapidFuzz 3.14.5):
//   PartialRatioScore("YANKEES", "NEW YORK YANKEES") = 1.0
//   PartialRatioScore("abc", "bc")                   = 1.0  (Pitfall-3 keystone)
//   PartialRatioScore("abc", "ab")                   = 1.0  (Pitfall-3 keystone)
func PartialRatioScore(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 6 algorithm surface (token tier — TokenJaccard)

Plan 06-04 lands TokenJaccard, the simplest Phase 6 algorithm — no
shared kernel beyond `Tokenise(s, DefaultTokeniseOptions())`, no LCS DP,
no three-way max, no sliding window. It computes the Jaccard
coefficient |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B| (Jaccard 1912 New Phytologist 11(2):37-50
p. 43, the SAME primary source as Phase 5's Q-Gram Jaccard) over the
deduplicated SET of tokens produced by Tokenise.

**Set vs Multiset distinction (LOCKED 2026-05-15):** TokenJaccard uses
SET semantics — token-presence is a binary signal, multiplicity is
collapsed via `map[string]struct{}`. This is DISTINCT from Phase 5's
Q-Gram Jaccard (`QGramJaccardScore`) which uses MULTISET semantics
over q-gram counts. The semantic divergence is intentional per
06-RESEARCH.md Pattern 8: a token appearing twice in the input doesn't
make it "more present"; q-gram presence is a multiplicity signal
because longer overlapping runs of identical q-grams indicate stronger
similarity. The KEYSTONE regression gate RV-TJ3 ("a a b" vs "a b" →
1.0) is pinned in four surfaces (algorithm godoc, unit test
`TestTokenJaccardScore_SetVsMultisetDistinction`, BDD scenario "set
semantics deduplicate repeated tokens", staging-golden entry); the
same inputs under multiset Jaccard would yield 2/3 ≈ 0.667.

**Both-empty STANDARD convention (LOCKED 2026-05-15):** TokenJaccard
follows the STANDARD catalogue both-empty → 1.0 convention. It does
NOT deviate like TokenSetRatio (which returns 0.0 per the LOCKED
RapidFuzz issue #110 bug-for-bug compatibility). The identity
short-circuit `if a == b { return 1.0 }` covers the both-empty case
directly; a post-Tokenise both-empty guard handles pure-separator
inputs where the raw strings differ but Tokenise produces empty slices.

**DET-03 intersection-cardinality (LOCKED 2026-05-15):** `|A ∩ B|` is
computed via integer-counter loop over the SMALLER set —
`for k := range small { if _, ok := large[k]; ok { intersection++ } }`.
The output is a scalar `int` (associative integer addition; map
iteration order does not affect the result); no slice is ever built
from set iteration on the output path. The union is then computed via
set inclusion-exclusion: `union := len(setA) + len(setB) - intersection`.
DET-03 satisfied without any sort.

**Hand-derived reference vectors (LOCKED 2026-05-15):** six
hand-derived `(a, b)` pairs RV-TJ1..RV-TJ6 per 06-CONTEXT.md §1b
LOCKED (no RapidFuzz cross-validation — the RapidFuzz cross-validation
corpus does NOT include TokenJaccard entries; the set-Jaccard formula
is unambiguous from Jaccard 1912). Each test case carries the formula
derivation in the test comment per algorithm-correctness-standards.

```go
// TokenJaccardScore returns the TokenJaccard similarity between a and b
// in [0.0, 1.0]: tokenise both sides using DefaultTokeniseOptions(),
// deduplicate each token list to a set, then compute |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|
// over the two sets (Jaccard 1912 set coefficient). Conventions:
// both-empty → 1.0 (STANDARD catalogue convention — NOT the
// TokenSetRatio 0.0 deviation); identity → 1.0 (a == b short-circuit);
// one-empty → 0.0. Symmetric across argument order — bit-for-bit
// equality, no float tolerance needed. SET semantics (deduped) —
// distinct from Q-Gram Jaccard's MULTISET semantics over q-grams.
//
// Reference vector:
//   TokenJaccardScore("a b c", "b c d") = 0.5
//     (set A={a,b,c}; set B={b,c,d}; |∩|=2; |∪|=4; J=2/4=0.5)
//
// Reference vector (set-vs-multiset KEYSTONE — RV-TJ3):
//   TokenJaccardScore("a a b", "a b") = 1.0
//     (left tokens dedup to {a,b}; right tokens {a,b}; J=2/2=1.0;
//      a multiset implementation would yield 2/3 here)
func TokenJaccardScore(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 6 algorithm surface (token tier — Monge-Elkan)

Plan 06-05 lands Monge-Elkan, the most complex Phase 6 algorithm — the
only one that takes a PLUGGABLE inner-metric AlgoID parameter. For each
token in A, take the maximum inner-metric similarity over every token
in B, then average the per-token maxima (Monge & Elkan 1996 KDD'96 §3).
With a fixed inner metric the directional variant is DIRECTION-SENSITIVE
— the same inputs in swapped order generally produce different scores.

Two surfaces ship (Phase 8.5 Q3 — symmetric-by-default rename):

- `MongeElkanScore`            — the SYMMETRIC default (arithmetic
                                 mean of the two directional scores;
                                 invariant under argument swap)
- `MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric`  — the directional variant (per-token-
                                 max-mean reduction over A's tokens)

The dispatch slot `AlgoMongeElkan` binds `MongeElkanScore` (the
symmetric default) with `AlgoJaroWinkler` as the default inner per
06-CONTEXT.md §4 LOCKED — so `AlgoMongeElkan` participates in the
standard `PropAlgorithmScore_Symmetric` property test set without
exemption. Consumers needing directional scoring call
`MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric` directly or use the Phase 8 Scorer option
`WithMongeElkanAlgorithm(weight, inner)` which forwards the
user-supplied inner AlgoID.

Phase 8.5 Q3 also REMOVED the inert `NormalisationOptions` parameter
from both surfaces — `Tokenise(DefaultTokeniseOptions())` is used
internally regardless; pre-Normalise inputs explicitly if you want NFC
/ diacritic stripping before tokenisation.

**Permitted inner allow-list (LOCKED 2026-05-15 — 14 entries per
OQ-4 RESOLUTION):** the inner AlgoID must be one of 9 character-tier
algorithms (AlgoLevenshtein / AlgoDamerauLevenshteinOSA /
AlgoDamerauLevenshteinFull / AlgoHamming / AlgoJaro / AlgoJaroWinkler /
AlgoStrcmp95 / AlgoSmithWatermanGotoh / AlgoLCSStr) + 4 q-gram-tier
algorithms (AlgoQGramJaccard / AlgoSorensenDice / AlgoCosine /
AlgoTversky) + AlgoRatcliffObershelp (the gestalt-pattern character-
tier algorithm — included per OQ-4 RESOLUTION because excluding it
would be arbitrary).

Phase 7 will EXTEND the allow-list ADDITIVELY by adding the 4
phonetic AlgoIDs (AlgoSoundex / AlgoDoubleMetaphone / AlgoNYSIIS /
AlgoMRA → 18 total entries). The allow-list itself is declared at
PACKAGE SCOPE in monge_elkan.go (NOT in init() per DET-13) and is
the single source of truth — when Phase 7 lands, planners ADD entries
to the map AND update the panic-test fixture (rejected: 9 → 5;
permitted: 14 → 18).

**Direct-call panic discipline (LOCKED 2026-05-15 per CONTEXT.md §3):**
passing AlgoMongeElkan (self-recursion infinite-loop guard per
RESEARCH.md Pitfall 4), any token-tier AlgoID (AlgoTokenSortRatio /
AlgoTokenSetRatio / AlgoPartialRatio / AlgoTokenJaccard — token-on-
token meaningless), or any Phase 7 phonetic AlgoID (AlgoSoundex /
AlgoDoubleMetaphone / AlgoNYSIIS / AlgoMRA — reserved for the
additive expansion) panics with the message

	"fuzzymatch: AlgoID <name> not permitted as Monge-Elkan inner metric"

where `<name>` is the canonical `String()` output of the AlgoID. The
exact message format is pinned by `TestMongeElkan_PanicMessageFormat`.
The Phase 8 Scorer option `WithMongeElkanAlgorithm` returns
`ErrInvalidAlgoID` for the same input — direct-call panic discipline
applies only to the direct surface where programmer error must fail
loudly.

**Asymmetry KEYSTONE (RV-ME6 / RV-ME-asym):** the RV-ME4 vs RV-ME6
input-swap pair with `inner = AlgoLevenshtein` is the load-bearing
direction-sensitivity regression detector on the directional surface.
RV-ME4: `MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric("alpha", "alpha beta gamma", Lev) =
1.0`; RV-ME6: `MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric("alpha beta gamma", "alpha",
Lev) ≈ 0.4667`. The difference (≈ 0.5333) is pinned by
`TestMongeElkanScoreAsymmetric_DirectionSensitive`,
`TestProp_MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric_DirectionSensitiveWhenTokenCountAsymmetric`,
AND the BDD scenario "MongeElkanScore is direction-sensitive". A silent
direction-swap regression surfaces as 3 separate test failures.

**DoS notice (06-CONTEXT.md §5 LOCKED):** on inputs with > 1,000
tokens per side, Monge-Elkan performs ~10^6 inner-metric comparisons.
With JaroWinkler inner (the default), each comparison is
O(token_length) — total cost approximates 10^7 character operations.
The three-part godoc block in monge_elkan.go (Complexity formula +
DoS notice paragraph + `BenchmarkMongeElkan_Pathological_1000Tokens`
fixture reference) documents the contract; consumers in untrusted-
input contexts must pre-validate token-count ceilings before calling.

**Reference vectors (hand-derived RV-ME1..RV-ME6 per 06-CONTEXT.md
§1b LOCKED — no RapidFuzz toolchain because RapidFuzz's default
inner may not match this project's JaroWinkler default):** RV-ME1
("user create" / "usr creating" with JaroWinkler ≈ 0.9125); RV-ME2
(identity → 1.0); RV-ME3 (disjoint Greek pair with JaroWinkler
≈ 0.6917); RV-ME4 (subset case with Levenshtein → 1.0); RV-ME5
(Unicode "café" / "cafe" with Levenshtein → 0.6); RV-ME6 (asymmetry
keystone — RV-ME4 swapped → ≈ 0.4667). Each derivation is reproduced
in the unit-test comment per algorithm-correctness-standards.

```go
// MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric returns the directional Monge-Elkan
// similarity between a and b under the given inner metric: tokenise
// both sides, then for each token in A take the max inner-metric
// similarity over every token in B; average the per-token maxima.
// Returns [0.0, 1.0].
//
// Direction-sensitive: MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric(a, b, inner)
// generally ≠ MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric(b, a, inner). For the
// symmetric default (invariant under argument swap) call MongeElkanScore.
//
// The inner AlgoID MUST be one of the 18 permitted inner metrics
// (9 character + 4 q-gram + AlgoRatcliffObershelp + 4 phonetic).
// Passing AlgoMongeElkan (self-reference) or any token-tier AlgoID
// panics with the documented message.
//
// Reference vector (RV-ME1):
//   MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric("user create", "usr creating",
//                             AlgoJaroWinkler) ≈ 0.9125
func MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric(a, b string, inner AlgoID) float64

// MongeElkanScore returns the symmetric default Monge-Elkan similarity
// (Phase 8.5 Q3 rename — the unsuffixed canonical surface): the
// arithmetic mean of MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric in the two directions
// (ME(a, b) + ME(b, a)) / 2. Order-independent by construction; the
// surface bound to dispatch[AlgoMongeElkan] per CONTEXT §4 LOCKED.
func MongeElkanScore(a, b string, inner AlgoID) float64
```

### Phase 7 algorithm surface (phonetic tier — Soundex)

Plan 07-01 lands Soundex, the first of four Phase 7 phonetic algorithms.
Soundex encodes a name as a 4-character phonetic code (1 uppercase letter
+ 3 digits). The **Knuth/Census variant** is used — H and W are transparent
skips (NOT separators). This is the distinguishing choice from the SQL/MySQL
variant (where H and W are separators).

**Variant gate (LOAD-BEARING):**
- `SoundexCode("Tymczak") == "T522"` (SQL/MySQL returns "T520")
- `SoundexCode("Ashcraft") == SoundexCode("Ashcroft") == "A261"` (H/W transparent)
- `SoundexCode("Robert") == SoundexCode("Rupert") == "R163"` (Knuth p. 393)

**Non-ASCII handling:** non-ASCII runes are silently dropped before encoding.
`SoundexCode("Müller")` sees `"Mller"` after dropping `ü`. For Unicode-aware
similarity compose with `Normalise` + diacritic stripping first, or use a
character-based algorithm (Levenshtein, Jaro-Winkler).

**Score semantics:** binary 0.0 / 1.0. Returns 1.0 if both codes are
non-empty and equal, 0.0 otherwise. Both-empty returns 1.0 (identity
short-circuit). One-empty returns 0.0.

**Dispatch:** `AlgoSoundex` (slot 23) binds `SoundexScore`. `SoundexCode`
is public but not dispatched (returns `string`, not `float64`).

**Monge-Elkan inner:** `AlgoSoundex` is permitted as a Monge-Elkan inner
metric (plan 07-01 adds it to `permittedMongeElkanInner`; the allow-list
grows from 14 → 15 entries).

```go
// SoundexCode returns the 4-character Soundex phonetic code for s using
// the Knuth/Census variant (Knuth TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.4). Returns "" for
// empty input or input containing no ASCII letters.
//
// Non-ASCII input handling: this algorithm operates on ASCII letters
// [A-Za-z] only. Non-ASCII runes (accented characters, emoji,
// combining marks) are dropped silently before encoding.
func SoundexCode(s string) string
```

```go
// SoundexScore returns 1.0 if a and b share the same non-empty Soundex
// code (Knuth/Census variant), and 0.0 otherwise. Identity short-circuit
// covers both-empty (1.0). One-empty returns 0.0.
//
// Non-ASCII input handling: this algorithm operates on ASCII letters
// [A-Za-z] only. Non-ASCII runes are dropped silently before encoding.
func SoundexScore(a, b string) float64
```

### Phase 7 algorithm surface (phonetic tier — Double Metaphone)

Plan 07-02 lands Double Metaphone (Philips 2000), the second of four Phase 7
phonetic algorithms. Double Metaphone encodes a name as **two** phonetic keys
(primary and secondary), each at most 4 characters from the charset `[A-Z0]`
where `0` represents the "th" (theta) sound. The secondary key captures
alternate pronunciations for names of ambiguous linguistic origin.

**Language-origin branches (5 total — mandatory checklist per CONTEXT.md §3):**
- **Germanic:** `DoubleMetaphoneKeys("Schmidt") == ("XMT", "SMT")`; `("Smith") == ("SM0", "XMT")`; `Score("Schmidt", "Smith") == 1.0` (shared XMT key)
- **Greek:** `DoubleMetaphoneKeys("Catherine") == DoubleMetaphoneKeys("Katherine") == ("K0RN", "KTRN")`
- **Romance:** `DoubleMetaphoneKeys("Pacheco")` primary or secondary contains `"PXK"` (Spanish branch)
- **Slavic:** e.g. `Sczepanski` produces non-empty keys (SZC compound)
- **Chinese-origin:** e.g. `Cheung` primary contains X or K (CHE trigger)

**Match rule (four-way):** `Score == 1.0` iff ANY of {primary_a == primary_b,
primary_a == secondary_b, secondary_a == primary_b, secondary_a == secondary_b}
where the matched key is non-empty. Otherwise 0.0.

**Non-ASCII handling:** non-ASCII runes are silently dropped before encoding.
For Unicode-aware similarity compose with `Normalise` + diacritic stripping
first, or use a character-based algorithm (Levenshtein, Jaro-Winkler).

**Dispatch:** `AlgoDoubleMetaphone` (slot 24) binds `DoubleMetaphoneScore`.
`DoubleMetaphoneKeys` is public but not dispatched (returns two strings).

**Monge-Elkan inner:** `AlgoDoubleMetaphone` is permitted as a Monge-Elkan
inner metric (plan 07-02 adds it to `permittedMongeElkanInner`; 15 → 16
entries).

```go
// DoubleMetaphoneKeys returns the primary and secondary phonetic keys for s
// using the Double Metaphone algorithm (Philips 2000). Each key contains at
// most 4 characters from the charset [A-Z0] where 0 represents the theta
// sound. Returns ("", "") for empty input or input with no ASCII letters.
//
// Non-ASCII input handling: this algorithm operates on ASCII letters
// [A-Za-z] only. Non-ASCII runes are dropped silently before encoding.
func DoubleMetaphoneKeys(s string) (primary, secondary string)
```

```go
// DoubleMetaphoneScore returns 1.0 if a and b share at least one non-empty
// phonetic key (four-way match: pp, ps, sp, ss) under the Double Metaphone
// algorithm (Philips 2000), and 0.0 otherwise. Identity short-circuit covers
// both-empty (1.0). One-empty returns 0.0.
//
// Non-ASCII input handling: this algorithm operates on ASCII letters
// [A-Za-z] only. Non-ASCII runes are dropped silently before encoding.
func DoubleMetaphoneScore(a, b string) float64
```

---

### NYSIIS (nysiis.go) — Taft 1970 / Knuth TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.4

**Algorithm:** NYSIIS (New York State Identification and Intelligence System).
Original Taft-1970 variant: 6-character truncation, each rule applied once.
Modified-NYSIIS (no truncation, iterate-to-fixed-point) is explicitly rejected.

**Source-Origin:**
- *Algorithmic origin:* Taft, R. L. (1970). Name search techniques. NYSIIS Special Report No. 1.
- *Canonical description:* Knuth, D. E. (1973). The Art of Computer Programming, Vol. 3, §6.4.
- *Note:* Taft 1970 is a NY State Special Report not available through academic publishers;
  Knuth's secondary description in TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.4 is the authoritative algorithm description.
- *Cross-validation:* jellyfish==1.2.1 (BSD-2-Clause) — reference vectors only.
- *MIT-licensed Go ports NOT consulted:* github.com/UjjwalAyyangar/go-jellyfish

**Load-bearing variant gate:**
`NYSIISCode("Catherine") == "CATARA"` (6 chars, NOT "CATARAN" which jellyfish returns).
`NYSIISCode("Brown") == NYSIISCode("Browne") == "BRAN"` (canonical Knuth pair).

**Non-ASCII:** non-ASCII runes dropped silently; ASCII [A-Za-z] only.

```go
// NYSIISCode returns the NYSIIS phonetic code for s, truncated to a maximum
// of 6 uppercase ASCII letters using the original Taft-1970 algorithm per
// Knuth TAOCP Vol. 3 §6.4. Output matches ^[A-Z]{0,6}$.
//
// Variant: original NYSIIS-1970, 6-character truncation, each rule applied once.
// NYSIISCode("Catherine") == "CATARA" (6 chars, NOT 7 as in modified-NYSIIS).
func NYSIISCode(s string) string

// NYSIISScore returns 1.0 if a and b share the same non-empty NYSIIS code
// (original Taft-1970 variant, 6-char truncation), and 0.0 otherwise.
// Both-empty returns 1.0 (identity short-circuit). One-empty returns 0.0.
func NYSIISScore(a, b string) float64
```

**Dispatch:** `AlgoNYSIIS` (slot 25) binds `NYSIISScore`.

**Monge-Elkan inner:** `AlgoNYSIIS` is permitted as a Monge-Elkan
inner metric (added to `permittedMongeElkanInner` in plan 07-03).

---

### MRA (mra.go) — Moore 1977 / NBS Tech Note 943

**Algorithm:** MRA (Match Rating Approach). Encodes names by removing non-leading
vowels and deduplicating adjacent identical consonants; truncates to first-3 + last-3
if pre-truncation length > 6. Comparison uses a 6-step procedure with Table A threshold
lookup and length-difference auto-mismatch gate.

**Source-Origin:**
- *Primary source:* Moore, G. B., Kuhns, J. L., Trefftzs, J. L., Montgomery, C. A. (1977).
  Accessing individual records from personal data files using non-unique identifiers.
  National Bureau of Standards, Technical Note 943.
  Available at: https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/Legacy/TN/nbstechnicalnote943.pdf
- *Cross-validation:* jellyfish==1.2.1 (BSD-2-Clause) — reference vectors only.
- *MIT-licensed Go ports NOT consulted:* github.com/UjjwalAyyangar/go-jellyfish

**Special API note:** `MRACompare` is the ONLY public function in the fuzzymatch
catalogue with a non-float64 return shape — it returns `(matched bool, simScore int)`
where `simScore` ∈ [0, 6] is the raw NBS Tech Note 943 similarity counter, exposed
per spec line 691 and CONTEXT.md §6 LOCKED.

**Load-bearing reference vectors:**
`MRACode("Byrne") == "BYRN"`, `MRACode("Kathrynoglin") == "KTHGLN"` (truncation gate).
`MRACompare("Smith", "Smyth") == (true, 5)` (sum=9, threshold=3).
`MRACompare("", "") == (true, 6)` (both-empty).
`MRACompare("Ad", "ZachariahMontgomery") == (false, 0)` (length-diff >= 3 auto-mismatch).

**Threshold Table A** (mraThresholdTable — package-level var, NOT init()):
sum ≤ 4 → threshold 5; 4 < sum ≤ 7 → threshold 4; 7 < sum ≤ 11 → threshold 3;
sum >= 12 → threshold 2 (clamped — RESEARCH.md Pitfall 7.C).

**Non-ASCII:** non-ASCII runes dropped silently; ASCII [A-Za-z] only.

```go
// MRACode returns the MRA (Match Rating Approach) phonetic code for s per
// NBS Tech Note 943. Output is ≤ 6 uppercase ASCII letters, ^[A-Z]{0,6}$.
func MRACode(s string) string

// MRACompare performs the full MRA comparison per NBS Tech Note 943.
// Returns (matched bool, simScore int) where simScore ∈ [0, 6] is the raw
// NBS similarity counter. THIS IS THE ONLY public function in the fuzzymatch
// catalogue with a non-float64 return shape (CONTEXT.md §6 LOCKED).
// Length-difference gate: |len(MRACode(a)) - len(MRACode(b))| >= 3 returns
// (false, 0) — NOT an error (per CONTEXT.md §6 LOCKED, Open Question 2).
func MRACompare(a, b string) (matched bool, simScore int)

// MRAScore returns 1.0 if a and b are an MRA match, 0.0 otherwise.
// MRAScore(a, b) == 1.0 iff MRACompare(a, b).matched (strict consistency
// invariant, property-tested by PropMRA_ScoreCompareConsistency).
func MRAScore(a, b string) float64
```

**Dispatch:** `AlgoMRA` (slot 26) binds `MRAScore`. `MRACode` and `MRACompare` are
public but not dispatched (the dispatch table is (a, b string) float64 valued).

**Monge-Elkan inner:** `AlgoMRA` is permitted as a Monge-Elkan inner metric
(added to `permittedMongeElkanInner` in plan 07-04 — FINAL Phase 7 state, 18 entries).

---

### Normalisation (normalise.go)

```go
// NormalisationOptions configures the Normalise pipeline. The struct is
// passed by value: per-call allocation is zero.
type NormalisationOptions struct {
    Lowercase       bool
    StripSeparators bool
    SeparatorChars  string
    SplitCamelCase  bool
    NFC             bool
    StripDiacritics bool
}
```

| Field | Type | Default | Effect |
|-------|------|---------|--------|
| `Lowercase` | `bool` | `true` | Fold ASCII A..Z via OR 0x20; non-ASCII via `unicode.ToLower`. |
| `StripSeparators` | `bool` | `true` | Replace runes in `SeparatorChars` and ASCII whitespace with a single space; collapse runs; trim. |
| `SeparatorChars` | `string` | `"_-.:/"` | Set of runes treated as separators. |
| `SplitCamelCase` | `bool` | `true` | Insert a single ASCII space at lowercase→uppercase transitions before folding. |
| `NFC` | `bool` | `true` | Apply Unicode NFC via `golang.org/x/text/unicode/norm`. |
| `StripDiacritics` | `bool` | `false` | Drop combining marks (Mn) via NFD → Remove(Mn) → NFC. Implicitly forces NFC ordering. |

```go
// DefaultNormalisationOptions returns the v1.x default configuration
// per docs/requirements.md §9 (D-03).
func DefaultNormalisationOptions() NormalisationOptions
```

```go
// Normalise returns s with the requested normalisation applied.
// Behaviour is idempotent for any fixed opts and produces byte-identical
// output across the five supported platforms. Empty input returns
// empty. Invalid UTF-8 in the input is replaced with U+FFFD.
func Normalise(s string, opts NormalisationOptions) string
```

### Tokenisation (tokenise.go)

```go
// TokeniseOptions configures the Tokenise splitter. The struct is
// passed by value: per-call allocation is zero.
type TokeniseOptions struct {
    Lowercase             bool
    SplitCamelCase        bool
    SplitConsecutiveUpper bool
    SeparatorChars        string
}
```

| Field | Type | Default | Effect |
|-------|------|---------|--------|
| `Lowercase` | `bool` | `true` | Fold each emitted token to lowercase after boundary detection. |
| `SplitCamelCase` | `bool` | `true` | Insert a token boundary at non-uppercase→uppercase transitions. |
| `SplitConsecutiveUpper` | `bool` | `true` | When set with `SplitCamelCase`, "XMLParser" → ["xml", "parser"]; otherwise → ["xmlparser"]. |
| `SeparatorChars` | `string` | `"_-.:/ \t\n\r"` | Set of runes treated as token separators; discarded from output. |

```go
// DefaultTokeniseOptions returns the v1.x default configuration per
// docs/requirements.md §10.
func DefaultTokeniseOptions() TokeniseOptions
```

```go
// Tokenise returns the tokens of s per the configured options. Empty
// or whitespace-only input returns a non-nil empty slice. Token order
// matches input byte order; consecutive separators do NOT produce
// empty tokens.
func Tokenise(s string, opts TokeniseOptions) []string
```

### Scorer type and NewScorer constructor (scorer.go — Phase 8 plan 08-02)

Plan 08-02 introduces the immutable composite weighted Scorer and its
NewScorer constructor with the LOCKED validation pipeline (missing-
threshold FIRST → empty-algorithms → defensive AlgoID bounds → weight
normalisation). Score and Match methods land in this plan; ScoreAll,
Threshold, Algorithms accessors, DefaultScorer, and DefaultScorerOptions
land in plan 08-03.

The Scorer is immutable after NewScorer returns — all fields are set
once and never written again. Score / Match are safe for concurrent use
from any number of goroutines without external synchronisation.

```go
// Scorer is the immutable composite weighted scorer. Obtain a *Scorer
// from NewScorer or DefaultScorer (plan 08-03). The zero-value is not
// usable. All methods are safe for concurrent use without locks.
type Scorer struct

// NewScorer constructs a composite Scorer from the supplied options.
// Validation pipeline (LOCKED per CONTEXT.md §2 + 08-RESEARCH.md
// Pitfall 3): options applied in order with first-error short-circuit;
// then missing-threshold (ErrMissingThreshold) FIRST; then empty-
// algorithms (ErrEmptyScorer); then defensive AlgoID bounds
// (ErrInvalidAlgoID); then dedup by last-write-wins; then weight
// auto-normalisation (sum-to-1 when WithNormaliseWeights(true), the
// default). On any error returns (nil, err).
func NewScorer(opts ...ScorerOption) (*Scorer, error)

// Score returns the composite similarity score in [0.0, 1.0] for
// (a, b). When the Scorer was constructed with normalisation enabled
// (default), Score applies Normalise(s, opts) to BOTH inputs ONCE
// before dispatching to each algorithm. The reduction loop uses the
// explicit (entry.weight * score) parenthesisation and AlgoID-sorted
// iteration order locked at NewScorer time, giving byte-identical
// results across runs, processes, and CI-matrix platforms. Safe for
// concurrent use without locks.
func (s *Scorer) Score(a, b string) float64

// Match returns Score(a, b) >= threshold (boundary inclusive). Threshold
// is the value supplied to WithThreshold during NewScorer. Safe for
// concurrent use without locks.
func (s *Scorer) Match(a, b string) bool
```

### Scorer accessor surface (scorer.go — Phase 8 plan 08-03)

Plan 08-03 lands the Scorer's introspection methods (Threshold, Algorithms)
and the per-algorithm breakdown method ScoreAll, along with the
ScorerAlgorithm data holder. ScoreAll's return type is the SPEC OVERRIDE
documented in CONTEXT.md §1 — `map[AlgoID]float64` (typed enum keys) rather
than the spec's `map[string]float64`. The api-ergonomics-reviewer signed off
on this override in plan 08-03's PR.

```go
// ScorerAlgorithm describes a single weighted algorithm in a Scorer's
// configured set. Returned by Scorer.Algorithms() as a fresh slice on
// every call so consumers can introspect, log, or display the Scorer's
// composition without coupling to unexported internals.
//
// ID is the typed AlgoID; use ID.String() for the canonical CamelCase
// display name. Weight is the POST-normalisation weight that the
// Scorer actually uses during Score's reduction loop.
type ScorerAlgorithm struct

// Threshold returns the match boundary stored at construction time
// (the value passed to WithThreshold, or 0.85 from DefaultScorer).
// Plain accessor with no side effects; safe for concurrent use.
func (s *Scorer) Threshold() float64

// Algorithms returns the configured weighted algorithm set as a fresh
// slice of ScorerAlgorithm values, in AlgoID-ascending order. Each
// element's Weight is the POST-normalisation weight. Fresh slice per
// call — consumers may freely mutate, sort, or filter without
// affecting subsequent calls. Safe for concurrent use.
func (s *Scorer) Algorithms() []ScorerAlgorithm

// NormalisationOptions returns the NormalisationOptions stored at
// construction time, along with a bool indicating whether the Scorer
// applies normalisation (false when WithoutNormalisation was the last
// normalisation option applied). The returned options are by-value —
// callers cannot mutate the Scorer's internal options through this
// surface; subsequent calls observe the original values regardless of
// any caller-side mutation. Used by the scan sub-package
// (github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch/scan) to canonicalise SuppressedPairs
// entries and build token buckets using the same normalisation
// pipeline the Scorer uses for scoring (per spec §8 + §12.3 + §12.5,
// as amended in Phase 9 plan 09-01 to resolve 09-RESEARCH.md Open
// Question 1). Safe for concurrent use; the Scorer is immutable
// after NewScorer.
func (s *Scorer) NormalisationOptions() (opts NormalisationOptions, applied bool)

// ScoreAll returns per-algorithm raw scores for the configured
// algorithm set as a map[AlgoID]float64.
//
// SPEC OVERRIDE: docs/requirements.md §8.3 specifies map[string]float64;
// this implementation returns map[AlgoID]float64 because AlgoID is a
// typed enum that the rest of the library exposes, giving consumers
// compile-time key safety. Use AlgoID.String() for snake_case display.
// The spec deviation is documented in CONTEXT.md §1 (Phase 8) and
// api-ergonomics-reviewer signed off on this override in plan 08-03's
// PR.
//
// Map iteration order is non-deterministic per Go map semantics. Map
// CONTENTS are deterministic byte-for-byte (per-algorithm scores are
// deterministic; see PropScorer_DeterministicAcrossRuns). Consumers
// requiring stable iteration order MUST sort the keys themselves —
// typically via fuzzymatch.AlgoIDs() then key-lookup.
//
// A fresh map is allocated on every call (spec §8.6). Pre-normalisation
// policy mirrors Score (CONTEXT.md §3 LOCKED). Safe for concurrent use.
func (s *Scorer) ScoreAll(a, b string) map[AlgoID]float64

// ScoreAndAll returns BOTH the composite score AND the per-algorithm
// breakdown in a single normalisation + algorithm pass. Used by
// consumers (notably scan.Check) that need both values to populate
// an emitted diagnostic record — calling Score and ScoreAll
// separately duplicates the per-algorithm work for every emission.
// Composite is bit-identical to Score(a, b); breakdown is bit-identical
// to ScoreAll(a, b). Both share the LOCKED pre-normalisation boundary
// (CONTEXT.md §3) and the DET-06 + Q11b FMA-defending reduction loop
// that produces cross-platform byte-identical output. Safe for
// concurrent use; pure function; freshly allocates the breakdown map.
func (s *Scorer) ScoreAndAll(a, b string) (float64, map[AlgoID]float64)

// DefaultScorerOptions returns a fresh, mutable slice of ScorerOption
// matching DefaultScorer's composition: six algorithms at equal raw
// weight (DamerauLevenshteinOSA, JaroWinkler, TokenJaccard,
// QGramJaccard, SorensenDice, DoubleMetaphone — per spec §8.5) plus
// WithThreshold(0.85). Consumers can append additional options or
// splice in WithoutAlgorithm to derive customised Scorers. Fresh slice
// per call — consumers may mutate it without affecting subsequent
// DefaultScorer() or DefaultScorerOptions() calls.
func DefaultScorerOptions() []ScorerOption

// DefaultScorer returns the opinionated default Scorer (spec §8.5
// composition: DamerauLevenshteinOSA + JaroWinkler + TokenJaccard +
// QGramJaccard + SorensenDice + DoubleMetaphone, equal weights,
// threshold 0.85). Cannot fail under normal operation; panics only on
// internal inconsistency (programmer error). Safe for concurrent use
// without locks. Use append(DefaultScorerOptions(),
// WithoutAlgorithm(X))... with NewScorer for "default minus X"
// compositions.
func DefaultScorer() *Scorer
```

### Scorer construction options (scorer_options.go — Phase 8 plan 08-01)

Plan 08-01 lays the functional-options surface for the composite weighted
Scorer. The Scorer type, NewScorer constructor, and Score / ScoreAll /
Match / Threshold / Algorithms methods land in plans 08-02 / 08-03; the
options below merely accumulate state into an unexported scorerConfig.

Every option validates its inputs at option-application time and returns
one of the documented sentinel errors on failure (ErrInvalidWeight,
ErrInvalidThreshold, ErrInvalidAlgoID). NewScorer short-circuits on
the first non-nil error.

```go
// ScorerOption configures a Scorer at construction time. Options are
// consumed by NewScorer in option-application order; the constructor
// short-circuits on the first non-nil error.
type ScorerOption func(*scorerConfig) error

// WithAlgorithm returns a ScorerOption that registers algo with the
// given weight using the algorithm's dispatch-table default parameters.
// weight must be > 0 (else ErrInvalidWeight); algo must be in range
// [0, numAlgorithms) with a populated dispatch entry (else
// ErrInvalidAlgoID).
func WithAlgorithm(algo AlgoID, weight float64) ScorerOption

// WithoutAlgorithm returns a ScorerOption that removes every previously
// accumulated entry matching id. If id is not currently present,
// returns nil without error — the silent-no-op semantic enables
// composition patterns like
//   append(DefaultScorerOptions(), WithoutAlgorithm(AlgoX), WithThreshold(t))
// even when AlgoX is not part of the default mix.
func WithoutAlgorithm(id AlgoID) ScorerOption

// WithNormalisation returns a ScorerOption that enables pre-comparison
// normalisation in the resulting Scorer. The Scorer applies
// Normalise(s, opts) to BOTH inputs once at its boundary; token-based
// algorithms continue to call Tokenise internally on the already-
// normalised string.
func WithNormalisation(opts NormalisationOptions) ScorerOption

// WithoutNormalisation returns a ScorerOption that disables pre-
// comparison normalisation. The Scorer passes raw inputs to every
// algorithm; token-based algorithms still tokenise the raw input.
func WithoutNormalisation() ScorerOption

// WithThreshold returns a ScorerOption that sets the Scorer's match
// threshold to t ∈ [0.0, 1.0]. Out-of-range values return
// ErrInvalidThreshold. WithThreshold is MANDATORY for NewScorer — if
// no WithThreshold option is present, NewScorer returns
// ErrMissingThreshold. DefaultScorer() bakes 0.85 in.
func WithThreshold(t float64) ScorerOption

// WithNormaliseWeights returns a ScorerOption that controls weight
// auto-normalisation at NewScorer time. true (default) divides each
// weight by the sum so Score ∈ [0.0, 1.0]; false preserves raw
// weights and waives the [0, 1] composite guarantee.
func WithNormaliseWeights(normalise bool) ScorerOption

// WithQGramJaccardAlgorithm registers Q-Gram Jaccard with weight and
// q-gram length n. weight must be > 0 (else ErrInvalidWeight); n must
// be ≥ 1 (else ErrInvalidQGramSize). Use instead of WithAlgorithm
// when n must differ from the dispatch default of 3.
func WithQGramJaccardAlgorithm(weight float64, n int) ScorerOption

// WithSorensenDiceAlgorithm registers Sørensen-Dice with weight and
// q-gram length n. weight must be > 0; n must be ≥ 1.
func WithSorensenDiceAlgorithm(weight float64, n int) ScorerOption

// WithCosineAlgorithm registers Cosine n-gram similarity with weight
// and q-gram length n. weight must be > 0; n must be ≥ 1.
func WithCosineAlgorithm(weight float64, n int) ScorerOption

// WithTverskyAlgorithm registers Tversky with weight and asymmetric
// parameters alpha + beta plus q-gram length n. weight must be > 0;
// n must be ≥ 1; alpha and beta must be ≥ 0. The α + β > 0 constraint
// is enforced at runtime by TverskyScore itself.
func WithTverskyAlgorithm(weight, alpha, beta float64, n int) ScorerOption

// WithMongeElkanAlgorithm registers Monge-Elkan symmetric with weight
// and inner-metric AlgoID. weight must be > 0; inner must be in
// [0, numAlgorithms) with a populated dispatch entry (else
// ErrInvalidAlgoID); inner == AlgoMongeElkan is rejected as
// trivial-recursion. The full 18-entry permitted-inner allow-list is
// enforced inside MongeElkanScore at Score time.
func WithMongeElkanAlgorithm(weight float64, inner AlgoID) ScorerOption

// WithSmithWatermanGotohAlgorithm registers Smith-Waterman-Gotoh
// local-alignment similarity with weight and affine-gap params.
// weight must be > 0. params validation is delegated to
// SmithWatermanGotohScoreWithParams (nonsense values produce a
// deterministic-but-meaningless score; no pre-validation here).
func WithSmithWatermanGotohAlgorithm(weight float64, params SWGParams) ScorerOption
```

### Sentinel errors (errors.go)

All sentinels are flat `errors.New` values. Discriminate via
`errors.Is(err, fuzzymatch.ErrX)`; never match the error message string.

```go
// ErrInvalidAlgoID indicates an AlgoID parameter does not match any
// registered algorithm in the dispatch table. Returned by the Phase 8
// Scorer option layer (WithAlgorithm, WithMongeElkanAlgorithm, …) on
// out-of-range or unpopulated AlgoIDs.
var ErrInvalidAlgoID = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid algorithm identifier")

// ErrInvalidInnerAlgo indicates a Monge-Elkan composite was constructed
// with an inner AlgoID outside the 18-entry permitted set. The Monge-
// Elkan formula composes a per-token inner similarity; token-tier
// algorithms and the MongeElkan self-reference are rejected. Direct
// MongeElkanScore / MongeElkanScoreAsymmetric calls panic with a value
// wrapping this sentinel (discriminate via recover() + errors.Is).
var ErrInvalidInnerAlgo = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid inner algorithm for Monge-Elkan composite")

// ErrInvalidQGramSize indicates a q-gram-based algorithm option (Phase
// 8 Scorer: WithQGramJaccardAlgorithm, WithSorensenDiceAlgorithm,
// WithCosineAlgorithm, WithTverskyAlgorithm) was constructed with
// n < 1. Direct algorithm calls (QGramJaccardScore, SorensenDiceScore,
// CosineScore, TverskyScore) panic with a value wrapping this sentinel —
// direct calls fail loudly on programmer error per docs/requirements.md
// §6.A LOCKED.
var ErrInvalidQGramSize = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid q-gram size")

// ErrInvalidTverskyParam indicates the Tversky algorithm option was
// constructed with α < 0, β < 0, α + β ≤ 0, or any NaN/Inf value.
// Returned by Phase 8 Scorer's WithTverskyAlgorithm; direct TverskyScore
// / TverskyScoreRunes calls panic with a value wrapping this sentinel.
var ErrInvalidTverskyParam = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid tversky parameter")

// ErrEmptyScorer indicates NewScorer was called without any algorithm
// option. Pass at least one WithAlgorithm option, or use DefaultScorer()
// for the opinionated six-algorithm composition. Returned by NewScorer
// (Phase 8) after the missing-threshold check passes.
var ErrEmptyScorer = errors.New("fuzzymatch: scorer has no algorithms (pass at least one WithAlgorithm option or use DefaultScorer)")

// ErrInvalidWeight indicates an algorithm weight passed to a Phase 8
// Scorer With*Algorithm option was ≤ 0. Weights must be strictly
// positive so that auto-normalisation has a positive divisor and the
// composite remains in [0.0, 1.0]. Returned at option-application time.
var ErrInvalidWeight = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid algorithm weight (must be > 0)")

// ErrInvalidThreshold indicates a WithThreshold value was outside the
// closed interval [0.0, 1.0]. Thresholds are compared against composite
// scores which the Scorer guarantees fall in [0.0, 1.0] under default
// weight normalisation. Returned at option-application time.
var ErrInvalidThreshold = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid threshold (must be in [0.0, 1.0])")

// ErrMissingThreshold indicates NewScorer was called without
// WithThreshold. The threshold is a calibration parameter with no
// universally-safe default, so the library refuses to guess. Pass
// WithThreshold(t) with t ∈ [0.0, 1.0], or use DefaultScorer() which
// bakes 0.85 in. This check fires FIRST in the validation pipeline.
var ErrMissingThreshold = errors.New("fuzzymatch: scorer threshold required (pass WithThreshold or use DefaultScorer)")

// ErrInternalInvariantViolated indicates a library invariant that should
// never fire in correct usage was violated — seeing it as a recovered
// panic value is unambiguous evidence of a library bug. The single
// current panic site is DefaultScorer() when its baked-in option
// pipeline fails validation. Discriminate via recover() + errors.Is.
var ErrInternalInvariantViolated = errors.New("fuzzymatch: internal invariant violated (library bug — please file an issue)")

// ErrInvalidSWGParam indicates a Smith-Waterman-Gotoh parameter struct
// (SWGParams) violates the documented sign-and-finite invariants:
// Match must be a finite, non-negative float; Mismatch, GapOpen, and
// GapExtend must be finite, non-positive floats. (SWGParams).Validate()
// panics with a typed-error value wrapping this sentinel when an
// invariant is violated. Discriminate via recover() + errors.Is.
var ErrInvalidSWGParam = errors.New("fuzzymatch: invalid Smith-Waterman-Gotoh parameters")
```

### Input validation and diagnostics (validate.go + warn_kind.go)

`fuzzymatch.Validate(a, b string) []Warning` is the consumer-facing input-quality diagnostic surface (Phase 8.5 Q4). It inspects the two input strings and returns a deterministically-sorted slice of `Warning` values describing problematic-but-non-fatal input shapes. Pure function, safe for concurrent use, never panics, never returns an error. Returns `nil` (not empty slice) when no warning applies — the VALIDATE-01 contract.

```go
// Validate inspects the two input strings and returns warnings
// describing problematic-but-non-fatal input shapes. Pure: no
// goroutines, no I/O. Safe for concurrent use. Never panics. Returns
// nil if no warnings apply (NOT an empty slice).
//
// Output is deterministically sorted by (Algorithm, Kind) — two
// successive calls with identical (a, b) return byte-identical slices.
//
// Per-WarnKind semantics (docs/requirements.md §11.5):
//   WarnEmptyInput              — a == "" or b == "" (AlgoIDAny scope)
//   WarnUnequalLength           — len(a) != len(b) (AlgoHamming scope)
//   WarnNoTokensAfterNormalise  — Tokenise output empty (5 token-tier algos)
//   WarnAllNonASCIIDropped      — input all-non-ASCII (5 ASCII-only algos)
//   WarnPathologicallyLargeInput — max(len) > 65536 (AlgoIDAny scope)
//
// See docs/algorithms.md#input-validation-with-fuzzymatchvalidate for
// the full reference and the Validate-then-Score idiom.
func Validate(a, b string) []Warning

// Warning describes a single input-quality observation. The Algorithm
// field scopes the warning to a specific algorithm (or AlgoIDAny for
// cross-cutting warnings). The Detail field is a human-readable
// elaboration; consumers MUST NOT parse Detail for programmatic checks
// — use Algorithm and Kind.
type Warning struct {
    Algorithm AlgoID
    Kind      WarnKind
    Detail    string
}

// WarnKind enumerates the 5 input-quality observation categories.
// Iota starts at 1 so the zero value remains "unset".
type WarnKind int

const (
    WarnEmptyInput WarnKind = iota + 1
    WarnUnequalLength
    WarnNoTokensAfterNormalise
    WarnAllNonASCIIDropped
    WarnPathologicallyLargeInput
)

// (WarnKind).String() returns the CamelCase label dropping the "Warn"
// prefix — "EmptyInput", "UnequalLength", "NoTokensAfterNormalise",
// "AllNonASCIIDropped", "PathologicallyLargeInput". Matches the
// AlgoID.String() convention (Phase 8.5 Q6b).
func (k WarnKind) String() string

// WarnKinds returns the full set of 5 WarnKind constants in declared
// order. Fresh slice per call; safe to mutate. Deterministic across
// runs and platforms.
func WarnKinds() []WarnKind

// AlgoIDAny is the sentinel AlgoID value used to scope a Warning that
// is not algorithm-specific. AlgoIDAny.String() returns "Any". The
// value (-2) is chosen deliberately: AlgoID(-1) is reserved as the
// canonical "invalid" sentinel.
const AlgoIDAny AlgoID = -2
```

**Validate-then-Score idiom** (the recommended pattern for code paths that audit input quality):

```go
warnings := fuzzymatch.Validate(a, b)
for _, w := range warnings {
    log.Printf("input quality: %s (%s): %s", w.Kind, w.Algorithm, w.Detail)
}
score := fuzzymatch.DefaultScorer().Score(a, b)
```

A runnable end-to-end programme lives at [`examples/validate-input-quality/`](examples/validate-input-quality/main.go).

### Collection scan sub-package (scan/scan.go — Phase 9)

The Phase 9 scan sub-package provides Layer 3 of the three-layer
architecture: a turnkey "which pairs in this collection are
similar?" API over the root *fuzzymatch.Scorer. Public surface
covers Item, Config, Warning, Kind (with KindWithinGroup +
KindAcrossGroups + String()), three sentinel errors, the
DefaultConfig opinionated helper, and Check. The full Check body
landed across Plans 09-01..09-06: validation pipeline (P0..P4
collect-all via errors.Join), within + cross-group passes,
token-bucket optimisation (bucketThreshold = 50, empirically
validated), three-rule suppression composition (Rule 1
SilenceLint + Rule 2 SuppressedPairs canonical-pair + Rule 3
cross-group identical-Name default — all pre-emission), raw-byte
lex canonicalisation of (NameA, NameB) with Groups + Tags
swapping in lockstep, deterministic sort.SliceStable on the
5-tuple sort key, and an in-line completeness assertion gated by
fuzzymatch.ErrInternalInvariantViolated. Import as
`github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch/scan`. The root package has no
dependency on scan; the sub-package depends on the root.

**SPEC OVERRIDE notes** (per 09-CONTEXT.md §1 D-01 + D-02; both
overrides signed off by api-ergonomics-reviewer in plan 09-01's PR):

- `scan.Warning.Scores` is `map[fuzzymatch.AlgoID]float64` (typed
  enum keys), NOT `map[string]float64` as the original spec §12.1
  line 1337 specified. Extends the Phase 8 ScoreAll override at §8.3
  and §8.6 for the same compile-time-safety rationale.
- The within/cross discriminator type is `scan.Kind`. The
  package-scoped form is unambiguous at the call site and avoids
  accidental symmetry with the root package's WarnKind.

```go
package scan

import "github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch"

// Item is one named thing the scanner compares. Required fields:
// Name (non-empty). Optional fields: Group (scopes the comparison;
// empty string is a valid single global group), SilenceLint (one-side
// suppression flag), Tag (opaque consumer data — never stringified
// into error messages).
type Item struct {
    Name        string
    Group       string
    SilenceLint bool
    Tag         any
}

// Kind classifies a scan.Warning's pair scope. SPEC OVERRIDE
// (Phase 9): renamed per 09-CONTEXT.md §1 D-02. Zero value is
// unspecified; documented constants start at iota + 1.
type Kind int

const (
    KindWithinGroup Kind = iota + 1
    KindAcrossGroups
)

// String returns the CamelCase label: "WithinGroup", "AcrossGroups",
// or the allocating "Kind(N)" fallback for out-of-range values.
func (k Kind) String() string

// Warning is one detected similar-name pair. NameA is the
// raw-byte lex smaller of the two item Names (D-06 uniqueness
// makes the (Kind, NameA, NameB, GroupA, GroupB) sort key a
// strict total order without any normalisation tiebreaker).
// SPEC OVERRIDE (Phase 9): Scores type map[fuzzymatch.AlgoID]float64
// per 09-CONTEXT.md §1 D-01.
type Warning struct {
    Kind           Kind
    NameA, NameB   string
    GroupA, GroupB string
    TagA, TagB     any
    Score          float64
    Scores         map[fuzzymatch.AlgoID]float64
}

// Config controls Check behaviour. Zero value is invalid (Scorer is
// nil → ErrNilScorer). The opinionated default 0.05 boost lives in
// DefaultConfig (SPEC OVERRIDE (Phase 9): migrated per 09-CONTEXT.md
// §2 D-04).
type Config struct {
    Scorer                       *fuzzymatch.Scorer
    CompareAcrossGroups          bool
    CrossGroupThresholdBoost     float64
    CompareIdenticalAcrossGroups bool
    SuppressedPairs              [][2]string
}

// DefaultConfig returns an opinionated Config bound to the supplied
// Scorer: CrossGroupThresholdBoost = 0.05, all bool fields false,
// SuppressedPairs nil. SPEC OVERRIDE (Phase 9): the 0.05 default
// location migrated here per 09-CONTEXT.md §2 D-04, mirroring Phase
// 8's DefaultScorer / DefaultScorerOptions pattern.
func DefaultConfig(s *fuzzymatch.Scorer) Config

// Check compares every pair per the Config and returns warnings
// for matches, sorted by (Kind, NameA, NameB, GroupA, GroupB).
// Two passes: within-group (always on; every i<j pair in the same
// Group is evaluated against the Scorer's threshold) and
// cross-group (opt-in via cfg.CompareAcrossGroups; pair scored
// against effectiveThreshold = min(1.0, scorer.Threshold() +
// CrossGroupThresholdBoost)). Three-rule suppression composition
// applied pre-emission: Rule 1 per-item SilenceLint, Rule 2
// canonical SuppressedPairs lookup, Rule 3 cross-group identical-
// Name default (when CompareIdenticalAcrossGroups=false). Multi-
// error semantics (D-03 + D-04 + D-05 + D-06): ErrInvalidItem and
// ErrInvalidConfig may be returned as errors.Join of multiple
// wrapped values; errors.Is walks Unwrap() []error (Go 1.20+).
// In-line completeness assertion panics with
// fuzzymatch.ErrInternalInvariantViolated if duplicate sort keys
// ever appear post-sort (unreachable under D-06-validated input).
func Check(items []Item, cfg Config) ([]Warning, error)
```

**Sentinel errors** (four-section godoc per Phase 8.5 Q4 template):

```go
var ErrNilScorer     = errors.New("scan: Config.Scorer is required")
var ErrInvalidItem   = errors.New("scan: invalid item")
var ErrInvalidConfig = errors.New("scan: invalid config")
```

## Sample programs

```go
// Quick start (Phase 1 primitives — Phase 2+ adds the algorithm functions).
package main

import (
    "fmt"

    "github.com/axonops/fuzzymatch"
)

func main() {
    opts := fuzzymatch.DefaultNormalisationOptions()
    opts.StripDiacritics = true

    // Normalise "Café Müller" → "cafe muller" (NFC + diacritic strip +
    // lowercase + separator collapse).
    fmt.Println(fuzzymatch.Normalise("Café Müller", opts))

    // Tokenise "User-CreateEvent.v2" → ["user", "create", "event", "v2"].
    fmt.Println(fuzzymatch.Tokenise("User-CreateEvent.v2", fuzzymatch.DefaultTokeniseOptions()))

    // Enumerate all 23 AlgoID values.
    for _, id := range fuzzymatch.AlgoIDs() {
        fmt.Println(id, id.String())
    }
}
```

## Determinism contract

`Normalise` and `Tokenise` are pure functions whose output is
byte-identical across:

- All five supported platforms (linux/amd64, linux/arm64, darwin/amd64,
  darwin/arm64, windows/amd64).
- All future patch releases of Go ≥ 1.26.
- All multiple invocations within a single process or across processes.

Verified by `testdata/golden/normalisation.json` and the
`TestGolden_Normalisation` cross-platform golden-file test (see
`docs/requirements.md` §11 and §13).

## Threat surfaces deliberately addressed

| Threat | Mitigation |
|--------|------------|
| Algorithmic complexity DoS via pathological input | Per-algorithm budgets in `docs/requirements.md` §14; native Go fuzz tests on every public function. |
| Determinism drift (map iteration / floats / init order) | `no-map-iteration` rule (DET-03); no transcendentals on output paths (DET-06); no `init()`-time tables (D-05); cross-platform CI matrix. |
| Supply-chain (dep introduced silently) | `make verify-deps-allowlist` asserts root `go.mod` has only `golang.org/x/text` as a non-indirect runtime dep. |
| Patent-encumbered algorithm slipping in | algorithm-licensing-reviewer gates every algorithm proposal; Metaphone 3 documented as the precedent. |
| API drift between `llms.txt` and code | `ai_friendly_test.go` parses every exported root-package symbol via `go/ast` and asserts each appears in `llms.txt`; failure fails CI. |

## Out of v1.0 scope

- **Needleman-Wunsch:** superseded by Smith-Waterman-Gotoh's local
  alignment, which subsumes the rare cases where global alignment is
  the right tool.
- **Soft-TFIDF:** would require a domain corpus dependency; out of
  scope for a stateless library.
- **Metaphone 3:** U.S. Patent 7,440,941. AxonOps declines patent-
  encumbered algorithms regardless of enforcement posture. See
  `docs/faq.md`.
- **ML / embeddings:** require model storage, runtime deps (torch /
  onnxruntime / transformers), and stateful caching. Out of scope.
- **Goroutine-parallel scan:** the Phase 9 scan sub-package is
  deliberately synchronous; concurrent invocation by the consumer is
  supported, but the library does not spawn goroutines internally.
- **Persistent caching:** not a library concern; consumers may wrap
  the public API with their own caching layer.
